# Script Safety Checklist: PR Checks Workflow

**Purpose**: Validate that script safety requirements in the spec and plan are complete, clear, consistent, and cover all edge cases for inline bash scripts in GitHub Actions, shell variable quoting, regex safety, exit code propagation, and filename handling.
**Created**: 2026-04-01
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Does the spec require `set -euo pipefail` for ALL inline bash scripts in the workflow, or only mention it generically? Are individual jobs (detect, test, validate-pr-title) each explicitly covered? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Constitution §II] -- **PASS**: FR-010 now states "Each inline script block MUST include `set -euo pipefail` as its first executable line." Plan §Shell Configuration confirms this applies to all jobs.
- [x] CHK002 - Is the `shell: bash` default requirement specified at the workflow level (via `defaults.run.shell: bash`) to ensure all `run:` steps inherit bash, or is it left to per-step configuration? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010] -- **PASS**: FR-010 now states "The workflow MUST set `defaults.run.shell: bash` at the workflow level."
- [x] CHK003 - Does the spec or plan define how `set -euo pipefail` is applied -- via explicit inclusion in each inline script block, via `defaults.run.shell`, or via a wrapper? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-010 specifies explicit inclusion in each inline script block. Plan §Shell Configuration documents the rationale: GitHub Actions' default template provides `-eo pipefail` but NOT `-u`; the explicit line adds `-u` and makes the contract visible.
- [x] CHK004 - Are requirements defined for what happens when `set -u` encounters an unset variable in an inline script (e.g., unset GitHub Actions context expressions)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, FR-013] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-013 requires all user-controlled context expressions to be passed via `env:` step-level declarations, which ensures they are always set before the script executes. Plan §Script Injection Prevention documents the pattern.
- [x] CHK005 - Does the plan's Technical Context section ("set -euo pipefail for all inline scripts" constraint) trace back to a specific spec requirement or only to the constitution? [Traceability, Plan §Technical Context] -- **PASS**: Plan traces to both FR-010 (spec) and Constitution Principle II. FR-010 now explicitly cites Constitution Principle II.

## Shell Variable Quoting in GitHub Actions Context

- [x] CHK006 - Does the spec or plan define quoting requirements for GitHub Actions context expressions (e.g., `${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}`) used inside inline bash scripts? [Spec §FR-013, Plan §Script Injection Prevention] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-013 requires intermediate env vars with double-quoted references. Plan §Script Injection Prevention documents the exact pattern: `"$TITLE"` (double-quoted).
- [x] CHK007 - Is the PR title injection risk addressed -- specifically, does the spec define how `${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}` is safely passed to the bash regex check to prevent shell injection from malicious PR titles? [Spec §FR-013, Security] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-013 requires env var intermediation. Plan and contract document the exact pattern with security rationale.
- [x] CHK008 - Does the workflow contract specify whether the PR title is passed via an environment variable (safe) or via direct interpolation in the script body (unsafe)? [Clarity, Contract §validate-pr-title] -- **PASS**: Contract now includes "Delivery" column specifying `env: TITLE:` and a Script Injection Prevention section.
- [x] CHK009 - Are quoting requirements for `${{ matrix.plugin }}` defined when used in path expressions like `${{ matrix.plugin }}/tests/run-all.sh`? [Plan §Script Injection Prevention] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Plan §Script Injection Prevention documents that `matrix.plugin` is safe for direct interpolation because plugin names are constrained to kebab-case by Constitution Principle I.
- [ ] CHK010 - Does the spec address the scenario where `${{ github.base_ref }}` contains unexpected characters when used in the git diff command? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- Note: `github.base_ref` is set by GitHub from the PR's target branch name, which is controlled by repository settings. Low risk but not explicitly addressed.

## Filename and Path Safety

- [x] CHK011 - Does the spec define how `git diff --name-only` output is processed when filenames contain spaces, newlines, or special characters? [Spec §FR-014] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-014 requires safe processing and documents the mitigation: extract first path component via `cut -d'/' -f1`, which isolates the kebab-case directory name.
- [x] CHK012 - Is the requirement for safe parsing of git diff output (e.g., using `--diff-filter`, NUL-delimited output with `-z`, or proper IFS handling) specified in the plan or contract? [Plan §Git Diff Output Safety] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Plan §Git Diff Output Safety documents the decision not to use `-z` (YAGNI) and the rationale: top-level directory names are kebab-case by constitution, making line-oriented parsing safe.
- [x] CHK013 - Does the plan define how top-level directory extraction from git diff output handles filenames with spaces in parent directories? [Plan §Git Diff Output Safety] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Plan documents that the script only uses the first path component (kebab-case, guaranteed safe), full file paths are never passed to commands that would break on spaces.
- [x] CHK014 - Are requirements specified for how the `test -f` check for `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` handles directory names with special characters? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-002] -- **PASS**: Plan §Git Diff Output Safety specifies `test -f "$dir/.claude-plugin/plugin.json"` with proper quoting around `$dir`. Constitution Principle I constrains directory names to kebab-case, eliminating special character risk.

## PR Title Regex Safety

- [x] CHK015 - Is the PR title regex `^(feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor|test)(\(.+\))?!?: .+$` evaluated for ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) vulnerability? Does the spec or plan document that the pattern is safe? [Spec §FR-015, Plan §ReDoS Analysis, Security] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-015 requires ReDoS evaluation and documents the safety analysis. Plan §ReDoS Analysis provides detailed 5-point analysis confirming no nested quantifiers, bounded greedy matches, no overlapping alternatives.
- [x] CHK016 - Does the spec define the regex engine used for PR title validation (bash `[[ =~ ]]` vs grep vs external tool) and its backtracking characteristics? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Plan §ReDoS Analysis] -- **PASS**: Research §5 specifies bash `[[ =~ ]]`. Plan §ReDoS Analysis identifies the engine as POSIX ERE (glibc `regcomp`/`regexec`) and notes it uses backtracking NFA.
- [x] CHK017 - Is the greedy `.+` inside the scope group `(\(.+\))` specified as intentional, and are its matching boundaries documented (e.g., does `feat(a]b): desc` match, and is that acceptable)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Plan §ReDoS Analysis] -- **PASS**: Plan §ReDoS Analysis scope note documents that `.+` matches any characters including `)`, and this is intentional per YAGNI.
- [ ] CHK018 - Does the spec define behavior for PR titles with very long strings (e.g., 1000+ character titles) in the context of regex matching? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- Note: GitHub limits PR titles to 256 characters. Plan §ReDoS Analysis references this platform constraint ("max ~256 characters").

## Exit Code Propagation

- [x] CHK019 - Does the spec or workflow contract define all possible exit codes from the detect job and their meanings? [Completeness, Contract §detect, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS**: Plan §Exit Code Semantics now defines detect exit codes (0 = completed, non-zero = script error via `set -e`).
- [x] CHK020 - Does the workflow contract's exit code table (0, 1, 2) for the test job trace to specific spec requirements? Is exit code 2 (test runner not found) covered by FR-012? [Traceability, Contract §test] -- **PASS**: Contract exit code 2 traces to FR-012. Plan §Exit Code Semantics includes the full table.
- [x] CHK021 - Does the spec define how a failure in the detect job propagates to dependent jobs (test)? Does the test job run if detect fails? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "The test job depends on detect via `needs: [detect]`. GitHub Actions' default behavior is that if a `needs` job fails, dependent jobs are skipped."
- [x] CHK022 - Is the requirement for exit code propagation from `bash tests/run-all.sh` through to the GitHub Actions job status explicitly specified, including whether `set -e` or explicit exit handling is used? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Shell Configuration] -- **PASS**: FR-010 requires `set -euo pipefail` which ensures exit code propagation from `tests/run-all.sh`. Contract §test documents "Exit code propagates as job status."
- [x] CHK023 - Does the spec define what exit code the validate-pr-title job returns on regex match failure vs. script error (distinguishing "title invalid" from "script crashed")? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "The script uses `exit 1` for a non-matching title and relies on `set -e` to propagate unexpected errors." Plan §Exit Code Semantics provides the full table.

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK024 - Is the Constitution Principle II requirement ("All bash scripts MUST begin with `#!/usr/bin/env bash`") reconciled with the fact that GitHub Actions inline scripts do not use shebangs? Does the spec acknowledge this difference? [Consistency, Constitution §II, Plan §Shell Configuration] -- **PASS**: Plan §Shell Configuration explicitly states: "Principle II requires `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang... These requirements apply to standalone script files. Inline GitHub Actions `run:` blocks are not standalone files... the shebang and chmod requirements are therefore inapplicable to inline scripts."
- [x] CHK025 - Is the Constitution Principle II requirement for "executable (`chmod +x`)" reconciled with inline workflow scripts that are not standalone files? [Consistency, Constitution §II, Plan §Shell Configuration] -- **PASS**: Same reconciliation in Plan §Shell Configuration covers both shebang and chmod.
- [x] CHK026 - Does the plan's statement "No standalone script files created (inline workflow scripts only)" align with Constitution Principle IV's requirement for Layer 4 unit tests for "all new bash scripts"? Is the exemption justified? [Consistency, Plan §Constitution Check, Constitution §IV] -- **PASS**: Plan Constitution Check §IV states: "No new bash scripts requiring Layer 4 tests. Workflow file itself is validated by running it on a real PR." This is consistent: inline workflow scripts are not standalone bash scripts, so Layer 4 does not apply.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK027 - Are requirements defined for the scenario where `git diff --name-only` returns an empty result (no files changed)? Is this distinct from "no plugin directories changed"? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-006] -- **PASS**: FR-006 handles empty plugin list. Spec edge case "What happens when the PR has no changed files (empty PR)?" addresses the zero-files case: "The plugin test job should skip gracefully." Both produce `[]` output.
- [ ] CHK028 - Are requirements defined for the scenario where the GitHub Actions runner cannot execute `bash` (e.g., shell not available)? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- Note: Spec Assumptions state "`ubuntu-latest` runner has `bash` available." This is a valid assumption for GitHub-hosted runners.
- [x] CHK029 - Are requirements defined for the scenario where `actions/checkout` fails (e.g., network error, permissions)? Does the workflow handle checkout failure gracefully? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "The step failure propagates via `set -e` and the job fails. No special handling is required beyond the standard `set -euo pipefail` error propagation."
- [ ] CHK030 - Does the spec define behavior when `origin/${{ github.base_ref }}` is not fetchable (e.g., base branch was deleted between PR creation and CI run)? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- Note: If the base branch is deleted while a PR is open, GitHub closes the PR and the workflow would not run. This is a platform-level guarantee.

## Non-Functional Requirements: Security

- [x] CHK031 - Does the spec or plan address the GitHub Actions script injection attack vector where `${{ }}` expressions containing user-controlled input (PR title, branch name) are interpolated directly into bash scripts? [Spec §FR-013, Plan §Script Injection Prevention, Security] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-013 requires env var intermediation. Plan §Script Injection Prevention documents the full pattern with security rationale and GitHub docs reference.
- [x] CHK032 - Are the minimal permissions requirements (FR-009) specified with enough detail to determine whether `pull-requests: read` or similar permissions are needed for reading the PR title from event context? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009] -- **PASS**: Contract §Permissions specifies `permissions: {}` for validate-pr-title. The PR title is available via `github.event` context which does not require explicit permissions -- it is part of the webhook payload.
- [ ] CHK033 - Does the spec define requirements for pinning `actions/checkout` to a specific SHA, and is a specific SHA or version documented in the plan or contract? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008] -- Note: FR-008 requires pinned versions. Contract §Action Versions specifies SHA pinning with version comments. The specific SHA is an implementation-time decision (latest stable at time of implementation), not a spec-level requirement.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- `[Gap]` markers indicate missing requirements that should be addressed in spec.md or plan.md
- All 13 original `[Gap]` items have been remediated via additions to spec.md (FR-013, FR-014, FR-015, edge cases) and plan.md (Script Safety Design section)
